title: Module 6 knowledge check
markdown: Bias sources, privacy habits, and related risks.

questions.prompt: A hiring model rates candidates from one neighborhood lower. The most likely root cause is:
choices: Bias present in the historical training data
The model is broken in a random way with no data link
Too little compute alone
Pure randomness with no connection to inputs
Paste health records into a consumer chatbot
Minimize data shared for the task
Follow your organization's approved-tool policy
Assume prompts may be logged unless told otherwise

questions.prompt: Prompt injection, in plain language, is when:
choices: Untrusted text tries to override a system's instructions
You buy more GPUs
A model refuses to answer any question
You verify a fact in an encyclopedia

questions.prompt: Fluent AI answers can still be factually wrong.
choices: True
False

questions.prompt: Managing harmful bias is emphasized as part of trustworthy AI in:
choices: The NIST AI Risk Management Framework
A random social media meme
The requirement that all AI must be free of any software bugs forever
AGI product manuals from 1990

questions.prompt: A practical response to energy and cost concerns is to:
choices: Use the smallest tool that meets the need and avoid unnecessary heavy calls
Always train a new foundation model for every email
Ignore organizational tool guidance
Treat every vendor benchmark as eternally true

questions.prompt: Which limitation is real for today's generative systems?
choices: No guaranteed ground-truth understanding of the world
They never reflect training-data bias
They cannot produce fluent text
They always cite primary sources correctly
